patients.name: Ayaan Gazali
patients.health_issue: 18-year-old male presenting following a cycling accident that occurred approximately 2 hours ago on the Castro Valley bike trail near Lake Chabot. Patient lost control of his bicycle at moderate speed while descending a paved incline, struck a raised curb edge, and was thrown forward off the bike landing directly on his left leg with significant rotational force. Patient reports hearing an audible crack at the moment of impact. He was wearing a helmet but had no additional protective gear. Bystanders assisted him immediately and called 911. Patient is ambulatory only with full support — unable to bear any weight on the left lower extremity. Pain is reported at 9 out of 10 in severity, localized to the mid-shaft of the left femur and extending into the knee joint, described as sharp, constant, and significantly worsening with any attempted movement or palpitation. Visible deformity is noted — left thigh appears shortened and externally rotated compared to the right side, consistent with a displaced mid-shaft femur fracture. Moderate to severe swelling and bruising are present across the anterolateral thigh, with skin intact and no open wound observed. Patient reports no sensation of numbness or tingling in the left foot, though capillary refill in the left toes is slightly delayed at approximately 3 seconds. Pedal pulse on the left is faint and requires careful assessment for vascular compromise. Given the mechanism, severity, and vascular concerns associated with this injury, the patient should be directed to Eden Medical Center in Castro Valley, CA, as the primary receiving facility — it is the closest Level III trauma-capable hospital with orthopedic surgical coverage and imaging available 24 hours. Patient has a 12-year history of Type 2 diabetes mellitus currently managed with Metformin 1000mg twice daily and Glipizide 10mg daily — relevant due to increased risk of impaired bone healing and infection. Blood sugar at time of incident unknown; patient reports he had not eaten since morning. Patient also has hypertension managed with Lisinopril 20mg daily and Amlodipine 5mg daily, and hypercholesterolemia managed with Atorvastatin 40mg nightly. Patient is allergic to Penicillin with a documented anaphylactic reaction in 2009 — critical for antibiotic selection if surgical intervention is required. BMI is approximately 31.4. Patient smoked for 22 years, quit 6 years ago — relevant to fracture healing timeline and anesthesia risk. Patient is a school bus driver and lives alone; post-operative mobility and home recovery support will require coordination. Emergency contact is daughter Priya Okafor at 6693609922. X-ray, CT of the left femur, and vascular surgery consult are recommended urgently. Patient was administered no medications prior to arrival. Last meal was approximately 7 hours ago. Patient is conscious, alert, and oriented x3 — GCS 15.
